free_text: LNL 7.5MeV Van de Graaff. Pulsed mode
Horizontal angular divergence of about 1.3 degr.
for the neutron beam. Proton pulses less than 1.5 ns.
The initial polarization of the neutron beam
emitted at 45 degr. in the 7-Li(p,n)7-Be reaction
Cylinder oriented with the axis parallel to
the neutron beam direction. Thickness chosen to
Pulsed mode with time discrimination to
correct for random background,and pulse shape
scintillator encapsulated in a glass cylinder,
80 cm in length and 30.8 mm in diameter.
Both ends in optical contact with XP2020
symmetrically with respect to the neutron beam
A stilbene scintillator at 400 cm from the
neutron source was used to monitor the neutron
intensity, and also to measure the total cross
Differential cross sections are compared with
results of optical model calculations. Nuclear
and Schwinger interactions are both shown to
Scattering cross sections are relative to the
Differential data are corrected for multiple
scattering and effects of finite geometry. For the
total cross sections the in scattering corrections
effects of the spatial extension of the detectors
Errors are given as standard deviations and
